title: Love-Song. (Translations From The Hebrew Poets Of Medaeval Spain.)
content:
"See'st thou o'er my shoulders falling,
    Snake-like ringlets waving free?
    Have no fear, for they are twisted
    To allure thee unto me."


    Thus she spake, the gentle dove,
    Listen to thy plighted love: -
    "Ah, how long I wait, until
    Sweetheart cometh back (she said)
    Laying his caressing hand
    Underneath my burning head."

Abul Hassan Judah Ben Ha-Levi. (Born Between 1080-90.)
meaning:
This evocative piece by Emma Lazarus, titled "Love-Song. (Translations From The Hebrew Poets Of Medaeval Spain.)", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
